# Repository-wide instructions

## Purpose
This repository contains the **Popforge code generation platform** and the **PopSalon business cluster** — the first product generated by that platform.

A **cluster** is a self-contained product generated by Forge from a YAML metadata folder. Each cluster has its own backend (.NET solution), frontend (Vue SPA), and docker-compose. Multiple clusters can coexist under `src/`.

Directory layout:
- `src/forge/Popforge.CodeGen/` — CLI tool (`forge`): generates a cluster's backend + frontend from YAML metadata
- `src/{cluster}/cluster.yml` — cluster-level metadata (name, namespace, version, languages). **Always at the cluster root, never inside `metadata/`.**
- `src/{cluster}/metadata/entities/` — one YAML file per entity (source of truth for domain model)
- `src/{cluster}/backend/` — .NET 8 solution (4 projects: Domain / Application / Infrastructure / Api)
- `src/{cluster}/frontend/` — Vue 3 SPA (Quasar, TanStack Query, OData)
- `src/{cluster}/docker-compose.yml` — local dev environment (postgres + api + frontend)
- `design-artifacts/` — product specs, UX flows, architecture decisions
- `demo-popsalon/` — read-only reference implementation (Neos/GroupeIsa proprietary, do not modify)

Always distinguish between:
- **Generator source code** (`src/forge/`) — treat as production software, it has its own tests
- **Generator templates** (`src/forge/.../Templates/*.scriban`) — source of truth for the shape of generated code
- **Generated output** (`src/{cluster}/backend/src/**/Generated/`, `src/{cluster}/frontend/src/**/Generated/`) — never edit directly; fix the generator or template instead
- **Manual code** — everything outside `Generated/` folders; forge never overwrites it

See `.github/instructions/tech-stack.instructions.md` for the full technology reference.

## General rules
- Prefer small, explicit, testable changes.
- Do not introduce hidden side effects.
- Preserve existing architecture unless the task explicitly asks for a redesign.
- When a file appears to be generated, prefer changing the generator or templates instead of editing generated output directly.
- Explain tradeoffs when modifying generation logic.
- Keep naming explicit and intention-revealing.
- Avoid magic strings and duplicated rules.

## Validation
- When changing generation behavior, also update related tests, snapshots, or golden files.
- Call out breaking changes in generated output.

## Copilot instruction best practices
- If a copilot instruction file is more than 4 000 characters, split it into multiple files otherwise it will not be considered in code review by GitHub.
